title: Harrisia platygona
intro: Harrisia platygona, also known as the queen of the night or night-blooming cereus, is a species of cactus native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Harrisia platygona include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Harrisia platygona is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally consumed to promote relaxation, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive discomfort.
taxonomy: Harrisia platygona is classified as a species within the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Harrisia, which is a group of epiphytic cacti native to the tropical regions of the Americas. Harrisia platygona is further classified as a species within the subgenus Pseudoharrisia. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and growth habits. Harrisia platygona's classification is an example of how scientists group and categorize plants according to their evolutionary relationships and shared traits.

Its extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and infections.
